title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: A two-cornered contest in the AIADMK stronghold Pappireddipatti constituency
content: Predominantly agrarian, one of the primary demands has been implementation of irrigation schemes to support agriculture, and setting up of agro-based industries to help marketing the produce.

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Development gaps and heritage identity shape Jayankondam constituency’s electoral narrative
content: The constituency reflects a blend of historical significance and present-day concerns, where issues such as tourism development, livelihood, infrastructure gaps and employment opportunities are expected to shape voter choices in the upcoming polls.

title: Baramati, Rahuri all set to conduct byelections on April 23
content: The Baramati bypoll holds significance, as these will be the first elections since the death of the eight-time MLA from the constituency, Ajit Pawar

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Is the ground under PMK shifting to favour TVK in Dharmapuri constituency?
content: The constituency is regarded as a PMK stronghold and a ‘safe-seat’ for Ms. Sowmiya. However, the ground in this PMK’s ‘safe-seat’ appears to be shifting  in favour of the TVK.

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Leemarose trying her luck in DMK’s pocketborough Lalgudi constituency
content: In the 12 Assembly elections held since 1971,  the DMK won the seat eight times; the constituency is blessed with rich groundwater resource making it one of the most fertile regions in Cauvery delta

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Uthangarai constituency braces for a two-cornered contest
content: A water harvesting project from Paambaru Dam envisioning a watershed of 4,000 acres, an irrigation project tapping into the run-off from KRP Dam to fill up 34 lakes in Barur are among the two significant demands of farmers, where farming is affected by drought.

title: West Bengal Assembly Elections: 152 assembly seats go to polls on April 23 in first phase
content: Massive central forces deployment, AI surveillance and political sparring mark eve of high-stakes first phase

title: Madras High Court denies permission for construction of multi-storey apartments at Parthasarathy Gardens in Alwarpet
content: Justice V. Lakshminarayanan quashes planning permission granted by Chennai Metropolitan Development Authority to RUIA Developers LLP

title: Demand to augment comfortable public transport buses along NH 66 to reduce dependance on private vehicles
content: Four/six-laning of NH 66 nears completion as demand grows to augment bus connectivity; experts call for more KSRTC buses, private participation, and a public transport policy to lessen reliance on private vehicles

title: Pilot project to plug leaks fails to reassure residents; P&T Apartment complex in Mundamveli continue to leak
content: Two coats of a leak-proof solution applied in one bathroom for a 10-day pilot; residents say it addresses only bathroom leaks, not rainwater seepage caused by structural flaws

title: Amaravati expansion gains pace with fresh land pooling, acquisition notices
content: State issues LPS and LAAR Act notifications in Guntur and Palnadu districts, focusing on infrastructure, flood mitigation and future airport plans

title: Samrat Choudhary approves funds for 75 ITI in first Cabinet meeting as Bihar CM
content: 75 ITI institutions in the state will be developed as state-of-the-art skill training centres; students will be taught modern, in-demand courses such as AI, semiconductors, sales and retail

title: Man killed in Bengaluru in dispute over pet pigeon
content: A 25-year-old private firm employee was allegedly murdered following a quarrel over a pigeon in Rajagopalanagar

title: Mahanadi tribunal gives last opportunity to Odisha, Chhattisgarh to settle river water dispute
content: The MWDT observed that no concrete proposal for settlement on any of the disputed issues was placed on record by two States

title: Woman who set male friend on fire in police custody in Bengaluru
content: A day after a man was burnt alive, the Byadarahlli police have taken Prema, 27, his female friend, into three days custody to ascertain the exact motive behind the murder

title: Panel set up to probe death at Bengaluru’s Victoria Hospital following ‘negligence’ allegation
content: A probe has been ordered at the State-run Victoria Hospital following allegations by a patient’s family that medical negligence led to his death

title: Party support for Independent candidate, an election strategy in which Jayalalithaa succeeded and MGR failed
content: Tamil Nadu has in the past witnessed two elections in which Independent candidates secured the backing of a mainstream political party — with strikingly different results. The two instances were separated by nearly 25 years

title: Former DGP R. Sreelekha’s protest slogan sparks debate
content: Several social media users point to the irony of a former police officer raising slogans against the force, while others criticised the language, which was deemed disrespectful

title: Bengaluru’s Yellow, Pink metro lines to spur realty prices: Report
content: These two metro lines are expected to spur real estate development across major micro markets; 5-7 million sq.ft incremental office demand is anticipated over the next two years in CBD, SBD 2 and Electronic City areas, predicts Colliers India

title: Pahalgam terror attack: Govt appoints Karambir Singh Nalwa as Special Public Prosecutor to lead trial
content: The NIA had filed a comprehensive chargesheet against six people and two terrorist organisations, Pakistan-based Lashkar-e-Taiba and its shadow outfit TRF, in connection with the terror attack

title: Bring in law to regulate use of mobile phone by children: panel
content: The Commission emphasised the need for the government to establish laws setting age limits and controlling screen time among children

title: Blue City a blend of infrastructure and environmental planning
content: The ambitious project of developing a network of reservoirs and canals in Amaravati is aimed at not only mitigating flood risks but also enhancing urban livability and tourism potential of the capital region, say officials

title: Thrissur fireworks accident: Disaster Management Department issues detailed guidelines for collection of DNA samples, burial of bodies
content: The guidelines outline detailed procedures to be followed in the case involving unidentified bodies, bodies that have been identified but not claimed, disputed identifications, and situations where only body parts have been recovered

title: Govt claims women winning unreserved panchayat seats in ‘significant numbers’
content: Parliament data show 10 States/union territories where quota percentage is exceeded, but limited to an excess of up to 5 percentage points; four States/union territories were not meeting the quota, while five had just enough

title: TN Assembly elections: Tirupattur’s Jawadhu Hills gets first green polling booth
content: Designed under the T.N. Climate Change Mission, the green polling booth consists of eco-friendly measures like coconut and bamboo leaves used for setting up shades

title: CMC College of Nursing organises graduation day in Vellore
content: R. Shankar Shanmugam, Registrar, Tamil Nadu Nurses and Midwives Council (Chennai) was the chief guest

title: 139 people cleared by tribunals after SIR exclusion allowed to vote in W.B. first phase polls
content: Inclusion is minuscule compared to 27 lakh deletions during the adjudication process, which involved over 60 lakh voters

title: Andhra Pradesh Ministers meet Singapore Home Minister Shanmugam on Day 2 of governance tour
content: Singapore Home Minister K. Shanmugam briefed the delegation on the city-state’s legal framework and policing; training sessions at NUS Cities and a visit to the Singapore City Gallery are lined up for the day.

title: M.P. farmers to get four times the market price compensation for agricultural land accusation, says CM Yadav
content: Yadav said that the move will benefit farmers whose land is acquired while also expediting the pace of various infrastructure projects, including irrigation schemes, roads, bridges, railway lines, and dams

title: More than 3.4 lakh election officials, EVMs dispatched to polling booths across T.N.
content: 3,60,068 polling personnel deployed to 75,000 polling booths as the State is set to vote today; CEO Archana Patnaik says 163 FIRs registered over social media posts violating Moral Code of Conduct

title: SP delegation en route to meet victim’s family allegedly attacked in Ghazipur
content: Locals question outside interference and politicisation of the death; Akhilesh claims Uttar Pradesh is witnessing worst phase of anarchy

title: Women’s groups seek 33% reservation in legislature
content: They accuse the BJP of using the Women’s Reservation Bill for political gain at a round table meeting in city

title: First phase of West Bengal elections - key constituencies, candidates, and important issues
content: 3.60 crore voters will decide the fate of 1452 candidates across the 152 Assembly seats spread around 16 districts of the State. Over 2400 companies of central forces have been deployed to conduct the high-stake polls.

title: 47 mandals in Andhra Pradesh record severe heatwaves, Kadapa sizzles at 44.4°C
content: Severe heatwave forecast has been issued for 95 mandals for April 23;  isolated places may witness light rain, thunderstorms and gusty winds until April 25, says IMD

title: Women’s panel chief flags lapses in sexual harassment inquiry at  Ayurvedic college
content: Complaint alleges threats to students; Internal Complaints Committee formed late and without external member, says Rayapati Sailaja

title: Congress OBC leaders want quota within women’s quota, seek rollout post caste census
content: The stance of the OBC leaders is at variance with the Congress’s  official position that has been pressing for an immediate roll out of women’s reservation law

title: 2006 Malegaon blast case: Bombay HC quashes charges against four accused
content: The case remains pending before the special NIA court and has seen a number of developments since its registration

title: Congress candidate moves SC against telecast of PM’s address
content: “The broadcast constituted a misuse of state machinery and official media for electoral gain, targeted at specific Opposition parties by name, and was delivered without any sanction, authority, or justification under Constitution or any law”

title: SHRC directs Kochi Corporation to cover open stretch of canal near Convent Junction
content: The Corporation secretary has been asked to inform the Commission of the action taken within three weeks

title: Prasar Bharati withdraws order to temporarily shift RNU from Dharwad
content: Union Minister Pralhad Joshi, who took up the issue with the competent authorities, thanks Union Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw and PM Modi

title: Tamil Nadu to vote across all seats, Bengal in phase 1; can Stalin, Mamata hold their bastions?
content: West Bengal and Tamil Nadu are set for high-stakes assembly elections on April 23, with intense campaigns by national and regional parties. Over Rs 1,000 crore in inducements have been seized, and central forces are deployed in West Bengal. Key contests include Nandigram and the DMK vs. AIADMK battle in Tamil Nadu, with superstar Vijay's new party adding a twist.

title: 'I am sorry': Former India selector recalls apologising to Rohit Sharma
content: Former selector Kris Srikkanth regrets omitting Rohit Sharma from India’s 2011 World Cup squad, saying he apologised for the decision. He explained it was due to team balance and preference for all-rounders. India, led by MS Dhoni, won the title, with Yuvraj Singh starring, while Rohit later became a top white-ball batter.

title: After $6.25B 'gift' to Trump Accounts, Michael & Susan Dell donate $750M
content: Michael and Susan Dell have pledged $750 million to the University of Texas at Austin for a new medical center and research campus. This significant donation will focus on integrating AI and advanced computing to improve patient care, research, and education. The project, expected to open by 2030, aims to enhance Austin's healthcare capabilities and advance medical innovation.

title: Meta to install tracking software on American employee's machines: Here is what the memo said
content: Meta is installing new tracking software on US employees' computers to capture mouse movements, clicks, and keystrokes for AI training. This initiative aims to improve AI agents' ability to perform work tasks autonomously, like using shortcuts. Meta assures employees the data won't be used for performance reviews, only for enhancing AI models.

title: Weeks after Bill Gates’ plan to shut Gates Foundation; CEO sends layoff memo to staff
content: The Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation is preparing to lay off up to 500 employees, approximately 20% of its workforce, as part of a cost-control effort. This move aligns with the foundation's plan to wind down by 2045 and aims to cap operating expenses at $1.25 billion while increasing program spending to a record $9 billion in 2026.

title: Maya Angelou quote for kids: Do your best, then do better
content: Maya Angelou's wisdom, particularly her quote 'Do the best you can until you know better. Then when you know better, do better,' offers a powerful life lesson. It champions honesty, growth, and continuous improvement, acknowledging that mistakes are part of learning. This message provides hope and empowerment, especially for young people navigating life's complexities.

title: Watch: Jerry Jones almost falls while rushing to Greet Bill Clinton at NFL draft
content: Dallas Cowboys owner Jerry Jones experienced a near-fall during a pre-draft press conference.  Former President Bill Clinton made an unexpected entrance. Jones's excitement to see his long-time friend led to the stumble.  The incident provided a lighthearted moment amidst draft preparations.  Their friendship stems from shared Arkansas roots and dates back to the 1990s.

title: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce send wedding invites; Blake Lively reportedly snubbed
content: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are all set to host the biggest event of the year as they gear up for their wedding on 3rd July in New York City as they remain private about it. As invites are being sent out to their high profile guests, it seems the billionaire pop star has made it clear what she feels about her former best friend, Blake Lively.

title: Lauren Kyle & Oilers Wives debut custom initial jackets for McDavid & Game 1
content: NHL Game 1 shifted the heads of other partners of the Edmonton Oilers, such as Lauren Kyle, when they attended the games in support of Connor McDavid and the team. The group were dressed in matching custom jackets with personalized initials, showing unity off the field. The visual was quick to spread over the internet and brought an additional layer of lifestyle and fan culture to the atmosphere of the playoffs in Edmonton.

title: UFC to break anthem rule for Trump-linked White House fight night, Dana White says
content: UFC Freedom 250 will take place on June 14, 2026, at the White House as part of US 250th independence celebrations and President Donald Trump’s 80th birthday. The seven-fight card features major title bouts, but the key change is UFC CEO Dana White breaking tradition by including a live national anthem performance for the first time in years.

title: Is Trump planning boots on ground amid ceasefire extension?
content: While extending a fragile ceasefire with Iran, the US is simultaneously deploying significant naval and ground forces to the Middle East. This dual approach suggests President Trump is preserving military options, including potential limited ground action, should diplomatic talks fail to secure new terms on Iran's nuclear program and regional security.

title: Trump cuts legal immigration 2.5x more than illegal crossings; students, H-1Bs hit
content: US legal immigration, particularly for students and skilled workers, has seen a steeper decline than illegal entries under the Trump administration. International students face increased visa hurdles and uncertainty, while H-1B visa issuances are pressured by higher fees and stricter rules. This trend risks impacting US innovation and competitiveness.

title: ‘PSL things in IPL!’ Social media erupts after Rishabh Pant’s costly error
content: Lucknow Super Giants' disciplined bowling was marred by a chaotic run-out attempt involving Rishabh Pant. A mix-up between Jadeja and Ferreira saw the latter stranded, but Pant fumbled the ball twice, allowing Ferreira to survive. This costly lapse, despite a strong bowling performance, highlights T20 cricket's unpredictable nature and the impact of missed opportunities.

title: 'Bhooth Bangla' day 5: Akshay Kumar film eyes Rs. 100 crore domestic milestone
content: 'Bhooth Bangla', directed by Priyadarshan and starring Akshay Kumar, Tabu, Paresh Rawal, Wamiqa Gabbi, Rajpal Yadav, Mithila Palkar and Asrani, is chasing the Rs. 100 crore India net milestone as it enters Day 6 of its theatrical run. With a worldwide gross of Rs. 118.12 crore, the horror-comedy has recovered strongly from its Monday dip with an 18.5% growth on Day 5. The film opened with Rs. 12.25 crore on Day 1, peaked at Rs. 23 crore on Day 3 and is now building toward a healthy first week total. Marking Akshay Kumar and Priyadarshan's reunion after 16 years, the supernatural comedy-thriller follows Arjun Acharya returning to his ancestral home to confront the demonic entity Vadhusur. Stay updated with all live Day 6 tracking, day-wise collections and the complete box office story of Bhooth Bangla.

title: Horoscope today: Daily astrological predictions for April 23, 2026
content: Horoscope Today: Read daily horoscope predictions for April 23, 2026. Know all about the astrological events and influences that will be affecting each of the 12 zodiac signs. Our astrologer has analyzed the movements of the planets and the alignment of the stars to bring you the most accurate and up-to-date horoscope predictions for the day ahead. Get ready to discover what the stars have in store for you today.

title: Sukesh writes to Jacqueline after approver plea, says ‘all is fair’
content: Alleged conman Sukesh Chandrashekhar has written a handwritten letter to actor Jacqueline Fernandez, reacting to her recent move to turn approver in the ongoing Rs 200 crore money laundering case. Addressing Jacqueline with affectionate nicknames like “Baby Botta Bomma” and “My Jackie Sukesh wrote an emotional note expressing his continued support.

title: Is Trump's Hormuz blockade a 'success'? 34 Iran-linked tankers bypass restrictions
content: US claimed that it holds "total control" over the Strait of Hormuz, calling it a success. However, reports reveal Iran-linked tankers are bypassing these restrictions. At least 34 tankers have continued operations, with some carrying significant oil volumes. This situation creates a complex environment for global oil trade. Tensions remain high as both sides assert control over the vital waterway.

title: 'Our batting is letting us down': Pant after 5th defeat in 7 matches
content: LSG skipper Rishabh Pant admitted their batting collapse is the main reason for their poor IPL 2026 run after a 40-run loss to Rajasthan Royals, their fifth defeat in seven games. He said the team must “look for answers inside” and improve shot selection and partnerships after being bowled out for 119 chasing 160, despite a strong bowling effort earlier.

title: IPL: Pant's misery deepens as LSG suffer shocking 40-run defeat to RR
content: Lucknow Super Giants suffered a shocking 40-run defeat to Rajasthan Royals after collapsing to 119 while chasing 160. Despite a strong bowling effort led by Mohammed Shami, LSG failed to handle pressure with poor shot selection. Mitchell Marsh’s 55 went in vain as wickets fell rapidly, triggered by Rishabh Pant’s dismissal. Rajasthan’s Ravindra Jadeja (43*) and Jofra Archer (3/20) played key roles in a clinical all-round win.

title: Iran war: Trump sanctions waiver or not - why India continues to buy Russian oil
content: In March, with the US sanctions waiver in effect, India has aggressively procured Russian crude, picking up millions of barrels. India has never officially said that it will stop buying Russian crude, and even when levels dropped after sanctions, Russia was still the biggest contributor.

title: Vinod Suryavanshi recalls struggle, humiliation as junior artist
content: Vinod Suryavanshi, who played the new Sachiv ji in Panchayat Season 3, recently opened up about his journey in the industry, revealing how he accidentally entered films and the struggles he faced as a junior artist. Vinod said that while the pay improved, the environment often came with humiliation. Even after moving towards acting, Vinod said he faced repeated rejection due to his appearance.

title: IRS officer's daughter murder: Accused arrested from Delhi's Dwarka
content: Delhi Police have arrested the main suspect, Rahul, in the murder of an IRS officer's daughter. The 22-year-old woman was found strangled in South Delhi, with investigators suspecting a mobile charging cable was used. The accused was apprehended at a hotel in Dwarka after being on the run.

title: Rishabh Pant’s horror run continues, dismissed for three-ball duck vs RR
content: Rishabh Pant's disappointing outing continued as he was dismissed for a three-ball duck against Rajasthan Royals, falling early in the chase. This early setback, coupled with a run-out in the first over, put immense pressure on Lucknow Super Giants. Despite a strong bowling performance restricting Rajasthan to 159, Pant's form remains a concern for LSG.

title: Gujarat-bound ship among two vessels seized by Iran near Hormuz
content: Iran has seized two ships in the Strait of Hormuz. One vessel was heading to Mundra port in Gujarat. This action escalates tensions in the vital oil chokepoint. The seizures follow recent incidents involving Indian-bound ships. The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps has taken custody of the vessels. Tensions remain high despite a ceasefire.

title: EC issues notice to Kharge over 'terrorist' remark, seeks reply within 24 hrs
content: Mallikarjun Kharge on Tuesday referred to PM Modi as a “terrorist” and later issued a clarification, in remarks expected to trigger political controversy during the ongoing election season. Kharge made the statement in Chennai at a press conference on the final day of campaigning for the Tamil Nadu assembly elections, scheduled for Thursday. He was criticising the opposition AIADMK over its alliance with PM Modi's BJP.

title: 'He’s not doing anything’: Cook wants Bethell to quit RCB mid-season
content: Former England captain Alastair Cook urges young batter Jacob Bethell to leave the IPL and return to Warwickshire. Cook believes Bethell, despite his T20 World Cup heroics, is wasting crucial development time on the bench with Royal Challengers Bengaluru. Consistent game time is vital for his progression, Cook argued, suggesting he could open for his county and aid England's future.

title: 'Mamata's Tolabazi has digital address': Amit Malviya jibes CM with 'PAY2TMC' post
content: BJP IT cell chief Amit Malviya launched a sharp attack on the Trinamool Congress, using '#PAY2TMC' and 'Tolabazi' to accuse the party of cash-for-service politics and widespread corruption. He cited wall posters in Kolkata and alleged irregularities in recruitment and public distribution systems, claiming the state's youth and poor have been 'robbed blind' ahead of the assembly polls.

title: Ali Raza scripts history, becomes youngest bowler from Pakistan to ...
content: Teen pacer Ali Raza etched his name in PSL history, becoming the youngest to claim a hat-trick. The 18-year-old achieved the feat for Peshawar Zalmi against Karachi Kings, finishing with 4/41. His spell dismantled Karachi’s lower order, restricting them despite Jason Roy’s 85. The milestone, first for Zalmi this season, highlights Raza’s immense promise and adds his name to an elite list of PSL hat-trick takers.

title: Month into new govt, Nepal home minister quits over links to bizmen under laundering lens
content: Nepal's Home Minister Sudan Gurung resigned amid scrutiny over his wealth disclosures and alleged business links with a businessman under money-laundering investigation. This marks the second ministerial setback for PM Balendra Shah's anti-corruption government within weeks, highlighting early challenges to its clean governance agenda.

title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi dismissed for 8 as RR’s top order falls apart vs LSG
content: Rajasthan Royals faced a dramatic early collapse against Lucknow Super Giants, with teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi enduring a rare failure. Mohammed Shami's early strikes and a brilliant catch by Digvesh Singh Rathi dismantled the top order. This puts immense pressure on captain Riyan Parag and Shimron Hetmyer to salvage the innings.

title: Lebanese Journalist Amal Khalil Killed In Israeli Strike
content: Rescuers said on Wednesday they had recovered the body of Lebanese journalist Amal Khalil after she was killed in an Israeli airstrike on a town in southern Lebanon.

title: 2 Journalists Injured In Israeli Airstrike In Lebanon
content: An Israeli airstrike wounded two Lebanese journalists on Wednesday while they were working near the border with Israel, their employer reported.

title: Putin Can Meet Zelensky Only To Agree Final Conflict Arrangements: Kremlin
content: Russian President Vladimir Putin can only meet Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskiy for the purpose of finalising agreements on the conflict, Russian news agencies quoted Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov as saying on Wednesday.

title: 11 Killed In Multi-Vehicle Crash In UP's Mirzapur
content: The collision occurred on a route descending from Dramandaganj valley towards the Lasoda area, where a vehicle lost control and crashed.

title: US-Iran War Live Updates: Iran Says Reopening Strait of Hormuz 'Not Possible' Under US Naval Blockade
content: US-Iran War, Ceasefire, Peace Talks Updates: The war is in its eighth week and has sent oil prices surging because of the closure of the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Two Resignations, Border Anger: Trouble Mounts For Nepal's New Government
content: Since rapper-turned-politician Balen Shah, 35, took office as prime minister, his government has been rocked by instability. Within just 26 days, two ministers have stepped down, raising concerns.

title: Nepal Home Minister Sudan Gurung Resigns In Weeks Over Financial Transactions
content: Sudan Gurung, a key figure in the Gen-Z protests, said he was leaving his post to ensure a "fair investigation".

title: 1,000 Crores And Counting: Record Seizures In Tamil Nadu, Bengal Before Polls
content: According to official data, Tamil Nadu has recorded total seizures worth Rs 599.24 crore, while West Bengal stands at Rs 472.89 crore so far.

title: Iran Says Reopening Strait of Hormuz 'Not Possible' Under US Naval Blockade
content: "Reopening the Strait of Hormuz is not possible amid a blatant violation of the ceasefire," speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf said on X.

title: "Only In West Bengal Do Dead People Vote": Swapan Dasgupta To NDTV
content: "After 15 years, there is mounting public dis-satisfaction and we have emerged as the only real alternative (to the Trinamool Congress), not the Left or the Congress. Only the BJP," Swapan Dasgupta said

title: RBI In Talks With Banks To Assess Risks Posed By Anthropic's Mythos: Report
content: RBI officials have, over the past fortnight, held consultations on Mythos-related risks with counterparts in the US Federal Reserve.

title: 'Much Appreciated': Trump Says Iran Won't Execute 8 Women After His Request
content: Trump had earlier said on social media that the women's release could work in Iran's favour in negotiations.

title: 3 US Presidents Rejected Netanyahu War Plan, Trump Agreed: Ex-Top Official
content: Former Secretary of State John Kerry said that the previous US presidents did not agree to go to war with Iran because they had not "exhausted all the remedies of peaceful process".

title: Pro-Hamas Candidates To Contest Gaza's First Poll Of Any Kind Since 2006
content: The vote in Deir al-Balah is part of Palestinian Authority municipal elections that Palestinians have cast as a display of national unity against a US plan for Gaza

title: 'Fish Politics' To Infiltrators: NDTV Exclusive With Trinamool's Shashi Panja
content: Bengal is set to vote in two phases -- April 23 and 29 -- with both parties intensifying efforts in what is expected to be a closely fought contest, particularly in South Bengal, where the TMC has so far contained the BJP's advance more effectively than in the north.

title: Enforce 'No PUC, No Fuel' Rule Strictly: Delhi Chief Minister Rekha Gupta
content: Delhi Chief Minister Rekha Gupta on Wednesday directed officials to strictly enforce the 'No PUC, no fuel' rule in the national capital to curb air pollution.

title: Air Force Jets Conduct Precision Drills On UP's Purvanchal Expressway
content: A diverse fleet of aircraft, including Jaguar, Mirage-2000, Sukhoi-30 MKI, C-295 and AN-32, alongside Mi-17 V5 helicopter, took part in the exercise.

title: Army chief visits US command in Hawaii to bolster security architecture across Indo-Pacific
content: general upendra Dwivedi made a high-level visit to the united states Army Pacific . he was granted a Guard of Honour at Fort Shafter, Hawaii . the commander undertook an aerial tour of Oahu Island .

title: Vaishnaw sets target for top rail officials after reality check of 'lightweight tools' for trackmen
content: railway minister asks how many of them have got 'lightweight tools' he says there is a sharp gap between what officials claim and the ground reality . railways has developed a mobile app which updates and alerts track workers about approaching trains more accurately .

title: NCW calls for setting up a national registry for tracking acid attack cases
content: the national commission for women has proposed a national acid attack case tracking registry linking FIR, medical care, compensation and rehabilitation . it has also sought the integration of online FIR systems with automatic relief and compensation triggers . the commission has submitted these recommendations to the ministries of home affairs, health and family welfare, women and child development and social justice and empowerment .

title: On Iran, Trump talks tough, then takes timeout
content: the decision has deepened a perception in Washington that the administration’s Iran policy is less a doctrine and more of a mood swing . he has argued that economic pressure is steadily eroding Iran’s position, and that backchannel diplomacy via Pakistan deserves more runway .

title: Buddha relics to go public for the first time in India
content: for the first time, sacred relics of Lord Buddha will be brought out for a public exposition in the country from may 1-15 . the government sees this ‘Sacred Exposition of the Holy Rels of Tathagata’, to be held in ladakh, as a strong message from India to the world for peace amid the west Asia crisis .

title: UK man admits raping a PIO woman in a religiously motivated attack
content: man admitted rape, robbery, strangulation and racially/religiously aggravated assault . he followed the woman off a bus in Walsall and forced his way into her home armed with a two-foot stick while she was upstairs in the bathroom . the woman tried to lock the door, but he barged in, turned the light off and assaulted her .

title: 'Greater Indian role in global conflicts possible': Rajnath warns neighbours on Pahalgam anniversary
content: defence minister said india has maintained a balanced diplomatic approach under prime minister Modi . he also praised the victims on the first anniversary of the attack at an Indian embassy in the german capital . the two sides discussed a range of bilateral security and defence issues .

title: 'Bodol noy, bodla chai': Didi flips 2011 election slogan
content: Mamata Banerjee gave a "bodol noy, bodla chai (revenge, not change)" call in 2011 . she demolished the decades-old left front fort in the state with historic win . "we will not allow Bengal to be set on fire like Lanka," she says .

title: Landlords illegally advertising for ‘Muslim only’ or ‘Hindu only’ tenants in London
content: listings feature phrases such as 'muslim only', 'for 2 Muslim boys or '2 Muslim girls' and 'Muslims preferred' the listings could breach the Equality Act 2010 in the UK, which prohibits discrimination based on religion, sex and race.

title: ‘For acts against India, the response is assured’: Army on Pahalgam anniversary
content: the Indian Army has issued a stern warning to the perpetrators of terrorism . in a post on X, the Army said, for acts against India, the response is assured . it shared a photo showcasing Operation Mahadev, with the tagline "It was only a matter of time" in red colour .
